questions.question: A student's test scores are 78, 85, 92, and 88. What is the mean of these four scores?
The median of five numbers is 12. If four of the numbers are 8, 10, 15, and 20, what is the fifth number?
questions.explanation: Arrange the four known numbers: 8, 10, 15, 20. The fifth number must be between 10 and 15 to make the median (middle value of 5 numbers) equal to 12. When ordered as 8, 10, 12, 15, 20, the median is 12.

questions.question: A data set has a mean of 50 and contains 6 numbers. If five of the numbers are 45, 48, 52, 55, and 60, what is the sixth number?
questions.explanation: The sum of all six numbers must equal 50 × 6 = 300. Sum of the five known numbers: 45 + 48 + 52 + 55 + 60 = 260. The sixth number is 300 − 260 = 40.

questions.question: The range of a data set is 24. If the smallest value is 7, what is the largest value?
questions.explanation: Range = largest value − smallest value. So 24 = largest − 7, which gives largest = 31.

questions.question: A data set contains the values 5, 9, 9, 12, and 15. Which measure—mean, median, or mode—is equal to 9?
questions.explanation: Mode = 9 (appears twice). Median = 9 (middle value when ordered). Mean = (5 + 9 + 9 + 12 + 15) ÷ 5 = 50 ÷ 5 = 10 (not 9).

questions.question: Two data sets have the same mean of 40 but different spreads. Set A has values 35, 40, 45, and Set B has values 10, 40, 70. Which set has a larger standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Set B has values much further from the mean (10 and 70 are 30 units away), while Set A's values are only 5 units away. Larger deviations from the mean result in a larger standard deviation.
